subject: App cannot be opened because it is damaged
description: Downloaded internal tool shows Gatekeeper 'damaged' dialog; user cannot launch.

ts_steps.title: Trash incomplete app
ts_steps.action: Move app to Trash; empty Trash of that item only.

ts_steps.title: Re-download from approved source
ts_steps.action: Download again; verify size/checksum if published; drag to Applications before first launch.

ts_steps.title: Quarantine remove if approved
ts_steps.action: Security-approved only: xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/App.app — document ticket.

ts_steps.title: Gatekeeper Open Anyway
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Privacy & Security — Open Anyway after failed launch if offered.

ts_steps.title: Right-click Open
ts_steps.action: Control-click app → Open → Open to record explicit user intent once.

ts_steps.title: Rosetta for x86 binary
ts_steps.action: If app is Intel-only on Apple Silicon, install Rosetta when prompted; do not frame as Intel Mac repair.

ts_steps.title: spctl assessment
ts_steps.action: spctl -a -vv /Applications/App.app — capture assessment for security team.

ts_steps.title: PKG reinstall
ts_steps.action: If distributed as PKG, reinstall via PKG rather than drag-copy.

internal_information: Quarantine, partial download, unsigned build. Rosetta is Apple Silicon compatibility, not Intel hardware troubleshooting.
